title: Portfolio Management Techniques: A portfolio manager holds two assets, A and B. Asset...
summary: Apply the CAPM to calculate expected return from beta, risk-free rate, and market return.
prompt: A portfolio manager holds two assets, A and B. Asset A has an expected return of 10% and a beta of 1, while Asset B has an expected return of 14% and a beta of 1.5. If the manager wants to create a portfolio with an expected return of 12%, what weights should be allocated to each asset?

options.text: Use the formula for expected return to find the correct allocation of weights
Slope of the security market line
Calculated using weights and standard deviations

solution: Use the formula for expected return to find the correct allocation of weights.
